mcqs.question: What is the primary goal of model-based learning?

options.text: To learn a model of the environment

mcqs.explanation: Model-based learning aims to learn a model of the environment to make predictions about future states and rewards.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between model-based learning and model-free learning?

options.text: Model-based learning uses a model of the environment, while model-free learning does not
Model-based learning uses a policy, while model-free learning uses a value function
Model-based learning uses a value function, while model-free learning uses a policy
Model-based learning uses a reward function, while model-free learning uses a model of the environment

mcqs.explanation: Model-based learning uses a model of the environment to make predictions about future states and rewards, while model-free learning does not.

mcqs.question: What is model-based value iteration?

options.text: A type of model-based learning where the agent uses the learned model to compute the value function
A type of model-based learning where the agent uses the learned model to compute the policy
A type of model-based learning where the agent uses the learned model to make decisions
A type of model-based learning where the agent uses the learned model to update the model

mcqs.explanation: Model-based value iteration is a type of model-based learning where the agent uses the learned model to compute the value function.

mcqs.question: What is the advantage of model-based learning over model-free learning?
mcqs.explanation: Model-based learning can be more sample-efficient than model-free learning.

mcqs.question: What is the disadvantage of model-based learning?
mcqs.explanation: Model-based learning can be more computationally complex than model-free learning.

mcqs.question: What is the application of model-based learning in robotics?

options.text: Learning a model of the environment

mcqs.explanation: Model-based learning has been applied to robotics to learn models of the environment.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between model-based reinforcement learning and model-based value iteration?

options.text: Model-based reinforcement learning uses the learned model to make decisions, while model-based value iteration uses the learned model to compute the value function
Model-based reinforcement learning uses the learned model to compute the policy, while model-based value iteration uses the learned model to make decisions
Model-based reinforcement learning uses the learned model to update the model, while model-based value iteration uses the learned model to compute the value function
Model-based reinforcement learning uses the learned model to compute the value function, while model-based value iteration uses the learned model to make decisions

mcqs.explanation: Model-based reinforcement learning uses the learned model to make decisions, while model-based value iteration uses the learned model to compute the value function.

mcqs.question: What is the application of model-based learning in finance?

options.text: Learning a model of the stock market

mcqs.explanation: Model-based learning has been applied to finance to learn models of the stock market.
